text:
[Insert name here] of [Insert home planet here], you have great [insert emotion here] and have been chosen by the [insert color here] lantern corps. 

Report to [insert headquarters here] for further training. Always remember: [Error: Power Ring AI must be updated]

name: Emotional Spectrum

options.label: [color=#8B0000]Infrared[/color]
options.description:
[weight=600]Emotion[/weight]: 
Unknown

[weight=600]Origin[/weight]: 
It is unclear what emotions you felt when the Infrared Power Ring found you, but as it is diametrically opposed to the [weight=500]Ultraviolet Lantern Corps[/weight], it is likely you were overly emotional when you should have been calm.

[weight=600]Corps Goal[/weight]: 
Undecided (Insufficient Leadership)

[weight=600]Balance[/weight]: 
Average

options.label: [color=Red]Red[/color]
options.description:
[weight=600]Emotion[/weight]: 
Rage

[weight=600]Origin[/weight]: 
You felt incredible rage, likely due to a great injustice done to you or a tragic loss you suffered, and were found by a Red Power Ring.

[weight=600]Corps Goal[/weight]: 
Punish Criminals

[weight=600]Corps Headquarters[/weight]: 
Ysmault (Sector 2814)

[weight=600]Balance[/weight]: 
Weak to [color=Blue]Blue[/color] and [color=violet]Violet[/color]. Empowered by [color=gray]Gray[/color]

options.label: [color=orange]Orange[/color]
options.description:
[weight=600]Emotion[/weight]: 
Greed

[weight=600]Origin[/weight]: 
The Orange Power Ring did not find you: You learned about it and were so greedy that you hunted it down and stole it. 

[weight=600]Corps Goal[/weight]: 
There is no [weight=500]Orange Lantern Corps[/weight] since no one with an Orange Power Ring would share. Instead, there is the [weight=500]Agent Orange[/weight] with the Power Battery and potential "recruits" trying to steal it.

[weight=600]Goal[/weight]: 
Acquire and keep valuables

[weight=600]Corps Headquarters[/weight]: 
Okaara (Sector 2818) or None (Deadbeat)

[weight=600]Balance[/weight]: 
Weak to [color=Blue]Blue[/color] and [color=violet]Violet[/color].

options.label: [color=#D5B60A]Yellow[/color]
options.description:
[weight=600]Emotion[/weight]: 
Fear

[weight=600]Origin[/weight]: 
You are capable of causing great Fear and were found by a Yellow Lantern Ring.

[weight=600]Corps[/weight]: 
The [weight=500]Sinestro Corps[/weight] was founded by Sinestro, a former member of the [weight=500]Green Lantern Corps[/weight] who was expelled for his tyrannical methods. The [weight=500]Sinestro Corps[/weight] has a similar goal to the Green Lantern Corps, but they believe in maintaining order with fear. 

[weight=600]Corps Goal[/weight]: 
Bring order to the universe

[weight=600]Corps Headquarters[/weight]: 
Qward (Sector -1) 
Korugar (Sector 1417) 
New Korugar or Rank (Mobile) 

[weight=600]Balance[/weight]: 
Strong to [color=Green]Green[/color], weak to [color=blue]Blue[/color]

options.label: [color=Green]Green[/color]
options.description:
[weight=600]Emotion[/weight]: 
Will

[weight=600]Origin[/weight]: 
You had great willpower and were found by a Green Power Ring

[weight=600]Corps[/weight]: 
The [weight=500]Green Lantern Corps[/weight] was founded by the Guardians of the Universe, a branch of Malthusians. The [weight=500]Green Lantern Corps[/weight] function as peacekeepers or police, maintaining order in the Oan Protectorate.  

[weight=600]Corps Goal[/weight]: 
Bring order to the universe

[weight=600]Corps Headquarters[/weight]: 
Oa (Sector 0) 
Mogo (Mobile) 

[weight=600]Balance[/weight]: 
Weak to [color=#D5B60A]Yellow[/color], empowered by [color=Blue]Blue[/color]

options.label: [color=Blue]Blue[/color]
options.description:
[weight=600]Emotion[/weight]: 
Hope

[weight=600]Origin[/weight]: 
When looking at the universe, you still had a sense of Hope. You were discovered by a Blue Lantern Ring, and after being thoroughly  tested, you were granted a Blue Lantern Ring.

[weight=600]Corps[/weight]: 
Guardians of the Universe, Ganthed and Sayd, formed the [weight=500]Blue Lantern Corps[/weight] to help the [weight=500]Green Lantern Corps[/weight] in their work.

[weight=600]Corps Goal[/weight]: 
Bring peace to the universe

[weight=600]Corps Headquarters[/weight]: 
Odym (Sector 2628) or Elpis

[weight=600]Balance[/weight]: 
Empowers [color=green]Green[/color], weakens [color=red]Red[/color], [color=orange]Orange[/color], and [color=#D5B60A]Yellow[/color]

options.label: [color=indigo]Indigo[/color]
options.description:
[weight=600]Emotion[/weight]: 
Compassion

[weight=600]Origin[/weight]: 
You were a sociopath with no compassion for the suffering you caused. The [weight=500]Indigo Tribe[/weight] found you and forced an indigo ring on you to induce compassion.

[weight=600]Corps[/weight]: 
The Green Lantern Abin Sur founded the [weight=500]Indigo Tribe[/weight] as an experiment on rehabilitation. The [weight=500]Indigo Tribe[/weight] is a nomadic group, searching out for suffering in the universe to spread or force compassion.

[weight=600]Corps Goal[/weight]: 
Rehabilitate Criminals

[weight=600]Corps Headquarters[/weight]: 
Nok (Sector 2814)
No Sector (Mobile)

[weight=600]Balance[/weight]: 
Average

options.label: [color=violet]Violet[/color]
options.description:
[weight=600]Emotion[/weight]: 
Love

[weight=600]Origin[/weight]: 
You felt great love for someone and were found by a Violet Power Ring.

[weight=600]Corps[/weight]: 
The [weight=500]Star Sapphire Corps[/weight] was founded by the Zamarons, a bunch of the Malthusians. The titular Star Sapphire was carved into rings, which were given to women who had been loved and spurned, providing them the opportunity for revenge. Over time, they also recruited women who suffered the loss of a loved one. Later, whoever was convenient.

[weight=600]Corps Goal[/weight]: 
Preserve Love

[weight=600]Corps Headquarters[/weight]: 
Zamaron (2813)

[weight=600]Balance[/weight]: 
Strong against [color=orange]Orange[/color] and [color=#D5B60A]Yellow[/color]

options.label: [color=#E75480]Ultraviolet[/color]
options.description:
[weight=600]Emotion[/weight]: 
Repressed Emotions

[weight=600]Origin[/weight]: 
You were feeling strong emotions, but you were suppressing them (for good or ill). You were discovered by Umbrax, who conscripted you with a Ultraviolet Power Ring.

[weight=600]Corps[/weight]: 
The [weight=500]Ultraviolet Power Lantern Corps[/weight] was created by Umbrax, an Emotional Entity imprisoned by the Source Wall. The Ultraviolet Lantern Corps seeks to assimilate the entire universe to the [weight=500]Ultraviolet Power Lantern Corps[/weight]

[weight=600]Corps Goal[/weight]: 
Enslave all life

[weight=600]Balance[/weight]: 
Average

options.label: [color=Black]Black[/color]
options.description:
[weight=600]Emotion[/weight]: 
None (Death)

[weight=600]Origin[/weight]: 
You had a violent death, but Nekron considered you a useful tool and used a Black Power Ring to reanimate you.

[weight=600]Corps[/weight]: 
The [weight=500]Black Power Lantern Corps[/weight] is the army of Nekron, the Lord of the Unliving, and is made of the deceased from all across the universe. Nekron is the physical manifestation of the concept of death as a cosmic entity. 

[weight=600]Corps Goal[/weight]: 
Extermination of all life

[weight=600]Balance[/weight]: 
Weak to [color=#808080]White[/color] (or a combination of colors)

options.label: [color=#808080]White[/color]
options.description:
[weight=600]Emotion[/weight]: 
All (Life)

[weight=600]Origin[/weight]: 
You were alive and therefore good enough for the White Ring.

[weight=600]Corps[/weight]: 
The [weight=500]White Power Lantern Corps[/weight] was created by the Life Entity to fight the [weight=500]Black Power Lantern Corps[/weight]. Once that was completed, the Life Entity had the White Lanterns do unusual tasks to maintain life in the universe.  

[weight=600]Corps Goal[/weight]: 
Maintain Life

[weight=600]Balance[/weight]: 
Strong against Black

options.label: [color=#CD7F32]Gold[/color]
options.description:
[weight=600]Emotion[/weight]: 
Unknown

[weight=600]Corps[/weight]: 
The [weight=500]Gold Lantern Corps[/weight] is supposed to be from a thousand years into the future and is said to be the combination of the [weight=500]Green Lantern Corps[/weight] and [weight=500]Sinestro Corps[/weight]. Not all the evidence backs this up.

[weight=600]Corps Goal[/weight]: 
Bring order to the universe

[weight=600]Balance[/weight]: 
Average

options.label: [color=#696969]Fractal Lanterns[/color]
options.description:
More of a phenomenon than a true Lantern Corps. The Fractal Lanterns spontaneously appeared due to fluctuations in the Emotional Spectrum. Anyone who feels a strong connection can generate a matching Power Ring; though some of the rings were of the seven basic colors, there are also some anomolous color and emotion combinations such as Sorrow, Digust, Envy, Jealousy, and Happiness. As their creation was anomalous, they have no Corps to lead them and no [weight=500]Power Batteries[/weight] to recharge with. 

[Counts as one of the seven basic colors or one of the 5 anomalous colors]

options.label: [color=#696969]Colorless Rings[/color]
options.description:
There have been several attempts to create Power Rings that access the entire Emotional Spectrum, such as the prototypical Phantom Ring or Universal Ring. In every case, this attempt ends badly; no mortal can handle the full force of the Emotional Spectrum. These Power Rings are hidden away, but people inevitably find them and think they are different. Though created by the [weight=500]Green Lantern Corps[/weight], none of the users of the Colorless Rings have stayed loyal. 

Does not count as any color. Any characteristic Power can be bought with a 2 point discount. Must take Emotional Instability without a gain to points.

options.label: [color=#696969]Power Prism[/color]
options.description:
The origin depends on the universe, but the Power Prism tends to be a crystal made by (or  made from) the Skrulls that functions much like a Power Ring. The Power Prism has the same powers as a standard Power Ring, but it has no connection to the Emotional Spectrum as there is only one prism, no analogue to a Lantern Corps.

[Does not count as any color. Must take crossover]

options.label: [color=#696969]Black Sun Orb[/color]
options.description: The [weight=500]Order of the Black Sun[/weight] is an anologue to a Lantern Corp who use hand-held orbs to manipulate the universe. They were secretly created by the Faction Paradox to sabotage the Time Lords of Gallifrey and are best known for the 4-D war with the Time Lords. Unlike the Lantern Corps, the Order of the Black Sun does not have a lofty goal and is only interested in maintaining their own power.

options.label: Two Colors
options.description:
You are so emotionally stable (or emotionally unstable) that you can wield two rings at once. 

[Choose a second color]

options.label: Yellow Belied
options.description: There was a bit of a mix-up; you do not cause great fear, you have great fear. You may be able to recharge your own ring, but other Yellow Lanterns will consider you a victim to feed from.

options.label: Compassionate Soul
options.description: There was a bit of a mix-up. You already had great Compassion before you got the Indigo Power Ring. Your Power Ring does not have to expand as much energy to impose compassion on you, which you can use yourself.

options.label: Close Enough
options.description: You cannot see Electromagnetic Radiation from 380 to 750nm, which is normally how the Emotional Spectrum is perceived and therefore manipulated. Instead, you use an analogue-like sound or radiowaves. This is slightly harder for other Lanterns to counteract.

options.label: [color=#CC7722]Ochre[/color]
options.description: Powered by [weight=500]Envy[/weight]

options.label: [color=#A24857]Mauve[/color]
options.description: Powered by [weight=500]Jealousy[/weight]

options.label: [color=gray]Gray[/color]
options.description: Powered by [weight=500]Sorrow[/weight]

options.label: [color=#B87333]Copper[/color]
options.description: Powered by [weight=500]Disgust[/weight]

options.label: [color=#AA336A]Pink[/color]
options.description: Powered by [weight=500]Happiness[/weight]

description:
All lantern Corps rely on the emotional spectrum to manipulate the universe. 

The emotional spectrum is a form of energy that is generated by sentient life and which corresponds to the visible spectrum of life. There are three negative emotions: ([color=red]Red[/color], [color=orange]Orange[/color], and [color=#D5B60A]Yellow[/color]), a neutral state ([color=green]Green[/color]) and the three positive emotions ([color=blue]Blue[/color], [color=indigo]Indigo[/color], and [color=violet]Violet[/color]). 

Beyond the two extremes is the invisible spectrum ([color=#8B0000]Infrared[/color] and [color=#E75480]Ultraviolet[/color]), which was hidden until recently. The further away the colors are from the midpoint of [color=green]Green[/color], the more the spectrum controls the user instead of the other way around. 

There is also [weight=500]Black[/weight] (the absence of the emotional spectrum) and [color=#808080]White[/color] (the combination of all of the emotional spectrum). Besides the existing colors, there are also anomalies like [color=#CD7F32]Gold[/color] and the colors of the **Fractal Lanterns** which does not seem to exist naturally. 

Different factions have have made use of the Emotional Spectrum for their own purposes. The Green Lantern Corps is the most famous, and deliberately or not, the other Lantern Corps follow their examples. Lantern Corps choose members (**Lanterns**) who epitomize their emotion, who are granted **Power Rings ** to manifest their powers using the Emotional Spectrum. Some Corps are considered enemies or allies, since some of the colors of the Emotional Spectrum are strong or weak to others. 

Pick **One Color** Your choice in color will change what later options you can pick and the cost of some of those options. Options with "**Any**" or "**Points**" can be chosen by any lantern.

name: Universe

options.label: Standard
options.description: Like most parallel worlds and timelines, your universe looks unremarkable. Sure, there are differences, but it is not noticeable on the cosmic scale.

options.label: Mirror Universe
options.description:
Like Earth-3 or Earth-148, everyone in your universe has reversed morality compared to a [weight=500]Standard[/weight] universe.

[Morals of any individuals mentioned in the CYOA are flipped.]

options.label: Rule 63
options.description:
Like Earth-11, everyone in your universe has the opposite sex compared to a [weight=500]Standard[/weight] universe.

[Any people mentioned in the CYOA are the opposite sex.]

options.label: Crossover
options.description: Like Earth-7642 or Eatth-1996, your universe formed from the melding of two other universes, leaving it strangely crowded.

options.label: Animal Universe
options.description: Like Earth-C or Earth-26, planets in your universe are inhabited by a mix of anthropomorphic animals.

options.label: Dinosaur Universe
options.description: Like Earth-27, planets in your universe are inhabited by a mix of prehistoric animals, particularly those from the Mesozoic.

options.label: Lego Dimensions
options.description: Like Earth-13122, everything in your universe is strangely rigid but easy to use in construction.

options.label: Cowboy Planet
options.description:
Like Earth-18 or Earth-1890, you come from a wild west world. 

Is the whole universe like that? Sure, why not.

subcategories.name: Optional
subcategories.text: Pick as many as you want

options.label: Duochrome Universe
options.description: In your universe, there is only your Lantern Corps and a Lantern Corps of a contrasting color. You will rarely encounter someone with Lantern powers and they will clearly only be enemies or allies.

options.label: Roy G. Biv
options.description: In your universe, there are only the Lanterns Corps of the basic seven colors. Though you may encounter Lanterns enemies and allies, they will be fairly predictable.

options.label: Copernican Principle
options.description: In your universe, Earth is *not* the most important planet in existence and is just one world out of many. Universe-ending crises can occur anywhere, whether it's in your [weight=500]Sector[/weight] or a trillion light years away.

options.label: Infinite Crises on Infinite Earths
options.description:
In your universe, Earth was the location of Crisis on Infinite Earths, Zero Hour, Final Crisis, Flashpoint, Dark Crisis, and many other universe-ending threats. 

That is not important, since *every* planet has a few of those every few years. You will be run ragged being involved in every crisis on every planet in your [weight=500]Sector[/weight]

options.label: Unemotional Source
options.description: In your universe, the Emotional Spectrum has no connection to emotions and the goals of the Lantern Corps have no emotional connection. Though Lantern Corps may have preferences when choosing potential Lanterns, these recruits do not need any strong emotional connection to use their Power Rings.

options.label: Shared Emotions
options.description:
In your universe, the Emotional Spectrum is limited, the more Lanterns there are, the less powerful each individual one is. Unless there is high attrition or mass recruitment, it is unlikely you will detect any difference.

[-5 Points with Light Into the Darkness]

[0 [color=orange]Orange[/color], [color=#696969]Power Prism[/color], [color=#696969]Black Sun Orb[/color], +1 Any]

options.label: Limited Emotions
options.description: In your universe, the Emotional Spectrum is limited and nonrenewable; each use of a Lantern power reduces this finite resource. Even wrose, the Emotional Spectrum is required for the structure of the universe and once it is depleted, the universe will die. It will be difficult to convince the other Lantern Corps that this is true or that *your* usage is justified.

options.label: Choose Doom
options.description:
Like Earth 3 or the Dark Multiverse, in your universe, evil will always triumph over good in the end. There can be short-term victories for good and different factions of evil can conflict, so success is never guaranteed. 

[Cost is reversed with Mirror universe.]

[-15 [color=#E75480]Ultraviolet[/color], [color=Black]Black[/color]]
[+15 [color=Green]Green[/color] , [color=Blue]Blue[/color], [color=indigo]Indigo[/color], [color=#CD7F32]Gold[/color], [color=#808080]White[/color]]
[0 Any]

options.label: Relict Population
options.description: In your universe, one of the species that is normally extinct (Kryptonian, Martian, or Czarnian) is not extinct. Perhaps sending their potential fate, they have expanded their territory. They may have a mix of morality, but billions of people with enough power to match a Lantern will greatly disrupt the balance of power.

options.label: Grounded Species
options.description:
In your universe, superpowered species are not that powerful. They can be harmed by excessive force and are pared down to their essential powers. Lanterns will have less trouble fighting them.

[[weight=500]Superpowered Species[/weight] cost 3 points less, to a minimum of -1 points.]

options.label: Overpowered Species
options.description:
In your universe, super-powered species are overpowered; it seems like they can only be stopped with their special weaknesses and they have more powers than they need. Lanterns can easily be overpowered when fighting them.

[[weight=500]Superpowered Species[/weight] cost 3 points more.]

options.label: Light into the Darkness
options.description: Your Lantern Corps is now defunct; it has been exterminated and you were chosen as a futile replacement. You have no support from other Lanterns and it is unlikely all the Corps enemies were destroyed at the same time.

description:
It is not clear which universe you are from, since there are 52 universes in the multiverse to search through. Or rather, you must be from one of the permutations in Hypertime in the only universe. Or rather, you are from one of the 52 universes or their echoes in the Dark Multiverse. Or rather, you could be from any of the multiverses in the omniverse. Or rather...

There are a few certainties of which universe you are from. Your universe must have planets in star systems in galaxies in galactic clusters.  Though there are physical laws of your universe, they can be defied with Superpowers. Some superpowers are caused by sufficiently advanced technology, much like your Power Ring. Some superpowers are caused by magic, which is often considered the enemy of the Lantern Corps. Some superpowers cannot be explained by technology or magic, but can still match the power of your Power Ring. Beyond these constraints, there are many potential divergences for your universe.

name: Species

options.label: Powerless Species
options.description: Your species has no powers and must rely on your Power Ring. This include species like: [weight=500]Humans, Korugaran, Ungarans, Rannians, Qwardians, Kreel, Xudarians, Talokites, Dominators, Kroloteans, The Reach, Gordanians, Exxorians[/weight]

options.label: Almeracian
options.description: Almeracians are an aggressive race who all hail from the planet Almerac. Their obsession with military dominance and eugenic science marks a long history of self-directed evolution. Following their selective breeding, they can use their psychic powers to enhance their own physique, use telekinesis, hypnotize people, use heat vision or teleport.

options.label: Amazon
options.description: Another branch of the [weight=500]Humans[/weight] of Earth: The Amazons are a race of warrior women. Depending on the universe and clan, they may be divine creations, refugee women who converted or reincarnated murder victims, and may live in Themyscira, Egypt, or the Amazon Jungle.  As semi-divine beings, the Amazons have improved physique and are immune to aging.

options.label: Atlantean
options.description: Another branch of the [weight=500]Humans[/weight] of Earth, the Atlanteans are survivors of the sunken city of Atlantis. They have split into kingdoms, ranging from nearly human to animalistic creatures, and ranging from pacifissts to xenophobic warmongerers. The pressures of the deep give them improved physiques, as well as the ability to breath underwater.

options.label: Bismollian
options.description: The Bismollian are a race of humanoids from the planet Bismoll in [weight=500]Sector 3493[/weight]. They evolved on a resource-poor world, leading them to develop the ability to digest any matter for food. To pair with this, Bismollians can produce enzymes to break down any item for digestion.

options.label: Bug
options.description: The Bugs were created by the natives of New Genesis as a failed bioweapon; they were planned to be released on Apokolips, but they escaped and adapted to New Genesis. They are a collectivist species, living in colonies that are led by a queen. They are a genetic mixture of insects and [weight=500]New Gods[/weight], giving osme of them enhanced physiques.

options.label: Coluan
options.description: The Coluans are green-skinned humanoids from the planet Colu in Sector [weight=500]1287[/weight]. Coluans are pioneers in advanced technology and have lifespans lasting for centuries, due to their advancements in genetic manipulations. The average Coluan has a Level-8 intelligence, far higher than average in other species.

options.label: Czarnian
options.description: Czarnians are a pale, humanoid species from Czarnia with a mixed history of peace and war (even within one universe). They were wiped out by [weight=500]Lobo[/weight], but more are occasionally found (and then are killed by Lobo). Czarnians are durable, have super strength, are capable of surviving in space and have such powerful regeneration abilities that they can regrow from a drop of blood.

options.label: Durlan
options.description: The Durlans are shapeshifters from Durla in [weight=500]Sector 700[/weight]. Once an unremarkable race, after the Six-Minute War, the Durlans were left without a true form. Though they can take the shape of nearly any object or creature, they mimic the form rather than their function (meaning that cannot have superpowers). More generally, they can survive in the vacuum of space or fuse together with other Durlans to make a larger object.

options.label: Grendan
options.description: The Grendans are the robots manufactured on Grenda in [weight=500]Sector 3009[/weight]. Though synthetic, their artificial intelligence is complex enough to grant them emotions, allowing them to wield Power Rings. They do not suffer from the frailties of organic life and are more easily repaired and modified.

options.label: H'lvenite
options.description:
The H'lvenites are a rodent species from H'lven in [weight=500]Sector 1014[/weight]. They live rustic lives, with limited technology They resemble anthropomorphic squirrels and are just as small (if arguably more agile than the average humanoid) .

[No cost change from [weight=500]Grounded Species[/weight] or [weight=500]Overpowered Species[/weight]]

options.label: Klaramaran
options.description: The Klaramarans (or the Faceless Hunters) are a race of range humanoids that are notable for their lack of any facial features. Though they are from Saturn, they are actually from a subatomic world in Saturn called Klaramar. They can increase their size to interact with the rest of the universe and have psychic powers to compensate for their lack of face. Besides that, Klarmans can fly and absorb and redirect elements.

options.label: Kryptonian/Daxamite
options.description: The Kryptonians are a nearly extinct species from the destroyed planet of Krypton in [weight=500]Sector 2813[/weight]. When exposed to yellow sunlight, Kryptonians have super speed, strength, and durability, flight, heat, and x-ray vision, super breath and frequently other powers, but they are poisoned by Kryptonite (the radioactive remains of Krypton). The Daxamites are an offshoot species, but thriving, xenophobic, and poisoned by lead instead of kryptonite.

options.label: Martin
options.description: The White Martians and Green Martians are two branches of the same race from Mars. White Martians are warlike and rare, but not extinct. Green martians are peaceful and nearly extinct. Martians have wide variety of powers derived from their shapeshifting and psychic abilities, such as strength, flight, phasing, enhanced vision, telepathy, telekinesis, and many others. Their only real weakness is pyrophobia, a weakness implanted in them by the Guardians of the Universe.

options.label: Meta-Gorilla
options.description: The more interesting species of Earth, the Meta-Gorillas of Gorilla City were enhanced mentally by a special event (varying between universes). Adding to their natural strength, the Meta-Gorillas were given super intelligence.

options.label: New God
options.description: The New Gods are natives of the planets of New Genesis and Apokolips, twinned planets outside the universe of contrasting morality. Genesisians are peaceful and have equalized strength, while the Apololiptians are cruel and have a hierarchy of power. The average New God is superior to mortals in every metric, though they are fatally poisoned by Radion.

options.label: Star Conqueror
options.description: Star Consquerors are a telepathic, starfish-like species. They can asexually produce Spawn, which latch onto other individuals and allow the main Conqueror to mind control them. Besides that, Star Conquerors can regenerate and fly in atmospheres or space. They are weak to extreme cold and quicklime.

options.label: Tamaranean
options.description: Tamaraneans are a colorful species from Tamaran in [weight=500]Sector 2828[/weight], known for their passionate natures. By absorbing solar energy, they have increased strength, speed, and durability, can fly and can survive in space. They can also assimilate languages by touch.

options.label: Thanagarian
options.description: The Thanagarians of Thanagar in [weight=500]Sector 2682[/weight] are a warlike, humanoid race, known for their improved physique and wings. Admittedly, in some universes, thee wings are artificial, but they are known for their skill with flight, despite naturally being flightless.

options.label: Vuldarian
options.description: Vuldarians are an ancient race of warriors from Vuldar. They were self-appointed guardians of their quadrant of the galaxy a billion years ago, but after war with the Tormock there were only stragglers and hybrids left. Vuldarians can reshape their bodies to form armor, melee weapons or ranged weapons. Besides that, they have increased durability and healing.

options.label: Yautja
options.description: Yautja are a species known for their ritualistic trophy hunting of other lifeforms. They have hunted many of the members of the Justice League. Yautja are strong enough that they hunt warriors from other species for sport.

description: Though there are endless number of species for the Green Lantern Corps to recruit from, there are some species that seem to be chosen repeatedly. Perhaps it's because of the natural powers of these species interact well with the abilities of the Power Ring. Perhaps it reflects an emotional depth in a species that can be exploited by the Power Ring. Perhaps, despite its claims otherwise, the Lantern Corps only care about people within a few million light-years.

name: Ring Powers

subcategories.name: Standard Powers
subcategories.text:
Do Lanterns from different Lantern Corps share powers because they are useful, because they mimic other Lantern Corps or because they are the most natural use of the Emotional Spectrum? It is hard to tell.

[You gain points by choosing to remove these powers]

options.label: Construct
options.description: The most archetypical power of the Lantern Corps, Lanterns can create hard-light constructs of anything they can imagine. The function is based on the intellect of the Lantern and the durability is based on the Lantern's mental strength. These Constructs dissipate into light when destroyed or dismissed. Lanterns can also create normal light projections to show images.

options.label: Flight
options.description: Lanterns can fly through the air at Mach 10, with higher speeds attainable with more energy and focus. Once they leave the atmosphere, they can increase their speed to faster-than-light speeds. The exact FTL method will depend on the universe, but the travel distance is the same with only the time duration reduced.

options.label: Onboard Computer
options.description: The Power Ring has a complex computer to assist the lantern This computer can scan through the electromagnetic spectrum for tracking or analysis. The computer can translate foreign languages (though it may have trouble with truly alien concepts) . Any lantern can use their Power Rings to communicate with the rest of the Lantern Corps without worrying about the lightspeed lag. For larger Lantern Corps, the Onboard Computer can access the main computer of the Corps, allowing easy research.

options.label: Atmospheric Protection
options.description: The Power Ring will protect the Lantern from dangerous environments, such as toxic atmospheres, changing water pressure or the vacuum of space. This works passively and Lanterns do not need to constantly focus on keeping themselves alive.

options.label: Energy Blast
options.description: Lanterns can focus light into a blast like a laser. This blast is a broad-spectrum burst, allowing it to harm most materials.

options.label: Uniform
options.description: The Power Ring can create a uniform that overlays the clothing the lantern is already wearing, with different Lantern Corps having more rigid dress-codes. Though a ring-generated Uniform is optional, a uniform is not; you need to show your Lantern Corps' logo somewhere, even if it is made from cloth.

subcategories.name: Characteristic Powers
subcategories.text:
Though some powers are found in all Lantern Corps, other powers are considered characteristics of specific Lantern Corps. You may be missing this power despite being a member of the Lantern Corps or you may have the power despite being in the wrong Lantern Corps.

[If a trait [weight=500]Gives Points[/weight] for your color, you can gain point to [weight=500]Remove[/weight] the power. If a trait [weight=500]Costs Points[/weight] for your color, you can pay points to [weight=500]Gives[/weight] the power.]

options.label: Emotional Empowerment
options.description:
Lanterns can recharge their rings with people feeling their matching color. White Lanterns can recharge using any of the seven basic emotions of the Spectrum.

[+3 [color=#808080]White[/color] or [color=#696969]Colorless Ring[/color], +1 Any]

options.label: Emotional Construct
options.description:
Your ring can read the mind of your target and create a [weight=500]Construct[/weight] to cause an emotional reaction. As not everyone has a visual cue for emotions, this power is not always reliable. 

[+1 [color=#D5B60A]Yellow[/color] or [color=Blue]Blue[/color], -1 Any]

options.label: Emotional Inducement
options.description:
Lanterns can infect people with their matching emotions, with less appropriate emotions for the situation requiring more energy. White lanterns can infuse people with any of the seven basic emotions of the Spectrum.

[-3 [color=#808080]White[/color], -2 Any]

options.label: Emotional Reading
options.description:
As the [weight=500]Death Empowerment[/weight] of Black Lanterns is more effective on people feeling strong emotions, Black Lanterns can sense strong emotions.

[+1 [color=Black]Black[/color], -1 Any]

options.label: Emotional Stability
options.description:
The Emotional Spectrum is powerful; perhaps too powerful for mortals. You can calm the minds of others, allowing them to act rationally. This can temporarily prevent [weight=500]Emotional Instability[/weight] or [weight=500]Overwhelming Emotions[/weight] in others, but can also limit the Lantern's powers.

[+2 [color=Blue]Blue[/color], -3 Any]

options.label: Charging Other Corps
options.description:
Blue Lanterns can spread hope by supercharging the rings of Green Lanterns in range, giving them up to 300% charge. They can charge other Power Rings to 200% by adjusting their Power Ring's settings. 

[+2 [color=Blue]Blue[/color], -3 Any]

options.label: Death Empowerment
options.description:
Every death a Black Lantern slightly increases the energy of their Power Ring. If the victim felt strong emotions before death, the energy gained is increased. 

[+3 [color=Black]Black[/color], -6 Any]

options.label: Energy Draining
options.description:
Agent Orange can drain the energy from the [weight=500]Constructs[/weight] of other Lanterns, recharging their own Power Ring and disintegrating the [weight=500]Constructs[/weight]. They can also drain magical constructs and technology, but less effectively. 

[+5 [color=Orange]Orange[/color], -7 Any]

options.label: Lantern Power Copying
options.description:
Indigo Lanterns can use the [weight=500]Characteristic Powers[/weight] of other Lanterns as long as they are close enough. As white light is a combination of the other colors, the same is true of White Lanterns. 

[+4 [color=indigo]Indigo[/color] or [color=#808080]White[/color], -6 Any]

options.label: Power Copying
options.description:
Ochre Lanterns, envious of the strength of others, can copy the superpowers of super-powered beings as long as they are close enough.

[+6 [color=#CC7722]Ochre[/color], -10 Any]

options.label: Technically Magic
options.description:
Red Power Rings were created with a mix of sufficiently-advanced technology and blood magic. Red Power Rings can bypass purely magical or technological issues.

[+0 [color=Red]Red[/color], -1 Any]

options.label: Reveal True Desires
options.description:
Star Sapphires can detect the deepest desires of others.

[+1 [color=violet]Violet[/color], -1 Any]

options.label: Empathic Healing
options.description:
Indigo Lanterns can heal people who feel great empathy and induce pain in people who lack empathy. (People in the middle feel nothing). Theoretically, other lanterns could do something similar with their emotions.

[+3  [color=indigo]Indigo[/color], -5 Any]

options.label: Healing
options.description:
Blue and White Lanterns are adept at healing other people, while other Lanterns may have difficulty. 

[+3 [color=Blue]Blue[/color] or [color=#808080]White[/color], -4 Any]

options.label: Undying Love
options.description:
If Star Sapphires encounter a casualty, they can use the life of the casualty's lover and their own body as a conduit to bring the person back to life. 

[+4 [color=violet]Violet[/color], -5 Any]

options.label: Resurrection
options.description:
The Life Entity can bring people back from the dead and White Lanterns can implore to bring back a specific target. The Life Entity is ineffable, so there is no guarantee of a resurrection (except for [weight=500]Dead[/weight] Black Lanterns, who are an affront to nature and are *always* resurrected)

[+8  [color=#808080]White[/color], -10 Any]

options.label: Regeneration
options.description:
Black Power Rings can continuously repair the damage their Lantern suffers. This Regeneration requires a large store of energy and cannot prevent damage from the Lantern's [weight=500]Weakness[/weight]

[+8 Black, -10 Any]

options.label: Zombie Animation
options.description:
Black Lanterns can reanimate corpses as servants. These zombies are largely mindless, but some have [weight=500]regeneration[/weight] as a Black Lantern.

[+6 Black, -10 Any]

options.label: Soul Trapping
options.description:
Agent Orange can trap the souls of people they kill, allowing them to recreate them as [weight=500]Constructs[/weight] that obey their will. They have a flicker of their old personality, but they obey the will of Agent Orange.

[+6 [color=orange]Orange[/color], -9 Any]

options.label: Stunning Shards
options.description:
When the [weight=500]Constructs[/weight] of Star Sapphires are broken, they shatter into a cloud of disorienting dust. Other powers can also create shards when completed, though with less certainty.

[+1 [color=violet]Violet[/color], -1 Any]

options.label: Teleportation
options.description:
Instead of traveling through FTL, Indigo Lanterns can create portals linking two points, allowing instant travel. The further the distance and the more matter to transport, the more energy it takes. 

[+6 [color=indigo]Indigo[/color], -9 Any]

options.label: Track Soul Mate
options.description:
Star Sapphires can track the person they love anywhere in the universe. If their soul mate is attacked, they can [weight=500]Teleport[/weight] instantly to their side.

[+6 [color=violet]Violet[/color], -9 Any]

options.label: Bloody Vomit
options.description:
Red Power Rings replace the blood of Red Lanterns with burning hot energy. Red Lanterns can vomit out this blood in combat, with it burning so hot it can burn in the vacuum of space. It is particularly good at fighting Lanterns, as they can molt [weight=500]Constructs[/weight]  and Power Rings and can prevent the [weight=500]Regeneration[/weight] of Black Lanterns.

[+5 [color=Red]Red[/color], -7 Any]

options.label: Antimatter Dimensional Breach
options.description:
Similar to the Sinestro Corps rings were designed by the Weaponers of Qward in the antimatter universe. As such, they need to have an easy way to transport to the antimatter universe and to convert to antimatter.

[+2 [color=#D5B60A]Yellow[/color] , -3 Any]

options.label: Lantern Infection
options.description:
The Blood of Red Lanterns can corrupt the Power Rings of other Lanterns and Ultraviolet Lanterns can convert Power Rings naturally. Not every Lantern is a good match, so this infection can be temporary in some circumstances. 

[+5 [color=Red]Red[/color] or [color=#E75480]Ultraviolet[/color], -7 Any]

options.label: Universal Infection
options.description:
Black Lanterns can bite other people, infecting them with the Black Emotional Light. This corruption will increase until the victim dies and is reincarnated as a Black Lantern.

[+6 [color=Black]Black[/color], -15 Any]

options.label: Crystallization
options.description:
Star Sapphires can encase people in crystal, which puts them in stasis. They can be infused with Love to transform them into Star Sapphire or the Love can be extracted for energy.

[+6 [color=violet]Violet[/color], -8 Any]

options.label: Smokescreen
options.description:
Sorrow Lanterns are surrounded by a constant cloud of smoke, even in the vacuum of space, making them harder to hit. Their [weight=500]Constructs[/weight] are also made up of the same smoke, though it does not make a large impact. 

[+1 [color=gray]Gray[/color] ,-1 Any]

options.label: Light Snuffing
options.description:
You can create a sphere of darkness around yourself. Other lanterns can emit light to pierce it, but even then the light will be feeble.

[+2 Black, -2 Any]

options.label: Excorcism
options.description:
Indigo Lanterns can pull malicious, possessive spirits out of people, with more powerful spirits requiring more energy. 

[+6 [color=indigo]Indigo[/color], -6 Any]

options.label: Emetic
options.description:
Disgust Lanterns can induce vomiting in others.

Well, okay, there's no proof of that, but wouldn't that make sense? 

[+1 [color=#B87333]Copper[/color], -1 Any]

subcategories.name: Special Powers
subcategories.text:
With special training, force of will, or desperate luck, some lanterns can use their Power Rings for special tricks.

[Optional: Pick as many as you want]

options.label: Telepathy
options.description: Your Power Ring allows you to communicate with other people using your mind. You can also use the onboard system of the [weight=500]Onboard Computer[/weight] to telepathically communicate across light-years.

options.label: Mind Reading
options.description: Your Power Ring can read the minds of of others; stronger wills will be able to resist.

options.label: Memory Modification
options.description: You can use your Power Ring to erase portions of an individual's memories. Stronger wills will be able to resist.

options.label: Precognition
options.description:
Known as the Emerald Sight in the Green Lantern Corps, you see brief glimpses of the near-future.

[-2 [color=Green]Green[/color], -3 Any]

options.label: Improved Physique
options.description: Instead of using your [weight=500]Constructs[/weight] to move and protect yourself, you infused your body with energy, improving your strength, speed, and durability. As long as the spirit is willing, the flesh will never be weak.

options.label: Age Modification
options.description: You can control your own aging with your Power Ring, making yourself younger or older. This cannot be done indefinitely. Tyran'r only lived for 10 billion years before his ring gave out.

options.label: Disguise
options.description: With the same principles as your Power Ring's [weight=500]Uniform[/weight], you can disguise yourself as another person of roughly equal size and shape. As long as you do not lose focus, this disguise will fool all the senses.

options.label: Phasing
options.description: Your ring allows you to phase through solid matter, with stronger matter requiring stronger mental strength. Forcefields will disrupt you if you try to bypass them though.

options.label: Sapient Constructs
options.description: You can create constructs of people which are capable of independent thoughts and actions. You can even grant them a fraction of your powers, though if they run out of energy from using these powers, they dissipate.

options.label: Tracking Signal
options.description: You can create an invisible sensor on an object, person, or location. If the sensor is disrupted, your Power Ring will alert you, no matter how far away you are.

options.label: Automatic Barriers
options.description: Your Power Ring will automatically set up barriers when you're in danger. Your [weight=500]Onboard Computer[/weight]  can't predict the future or understand every circumstance, so this barrier might not activate at the right time.

options.label: Deputizing
options.description: Your ring can create a copy of itself, allowing you to turn someone into a Lantern for 24 hours. This duplicate ring will use the same power source as you, so don't create copies lightly.

options.label: Phantom Zone Portal
options.description: The Phantom Zone is an inter-dimensional realm outside the normal space/time continuum which has been used as a prison by many factions. You can open a portal to the Phantom Zone, perhaps as a jail or an escape method.

options.label: Long-Range Actions
options.description: Normally, Lanterns use light, which moves at light-speed. By focusing carefully, you can see things at a distance without dealing with the light-speed lag. Creations like [weight=500]Constructs[/weight] and [weight=500]Energy Blasts[/weight] will appear instantly.

options.label: Invisibility
options.description: You can turn objects or people invisible. You can decide when they return to visibility, though they will automatically return if your Power Ring runs out of power.

options.label: Discolored Constructs
options.description: Your [weight=500]Constructs[/weight] do not have to be your one color. This might be too fiddly to do in combat, but you can create color-accurate items for traps.

options.label: Mental Block
options.description: Instead of turning an object or area [weight=500]Invisible[/weight], you surround it in a field that makes people forget it exists. This does not work on non-thinking recording devices and will break down if you cause a fuss.

options.label: Inanimate Animation
options.description: You can use your ring to animate objects. Unlike creating a [weight=500]Construct[/weight], the durability is a property of matter instead of your emotional state. If you lose control, the object becomes inanimate instead of dissipating.

options.label: Storage System
options.description: Your Power Ring can convert matter into energy and store it (simple matter being the easiest, Willful life bring the hardest} . If you have [weight=500]Matter Generation[/weight], you can recreate these items later. Otherwise, you can recreate them as [weight=500]Constructs[/weight] or add the information to your  [weight=500]Onboard Computer[/weight].

options.label: Matter Generation
options.description: Instead of creating hard-light [weight=500]Constructs[/weight], you turn energy into matter. Keep these structures simple or small or you will deplete all your energy storage at once.

options.label: Electricity Generation
options.description: Instead of just an  [weight=500]Energy Blast[/weight], you can generate an electrical current.

options.label: Radiation Generation
options.description: Instead of just visible light, you can shift your Power Ring's emissions to other parts of the electromagnetic spectrum, including dangerous ionizing radiation.

options.label: Fire Generation
options.description: Your Power Ring can produce fire without fuel. This fire can burn in the wrong atmosphere or no atmosphere if you keep your focus.

options.label: Temperature Control
options.description: Instead of [weight=500]Generating Fire[/weight], you can directly increase or decrease the temperature of an object. More effort can increase the speed and intensity of temperature change.

options.label: Animal Communication
options.description: Though Power RIngs can translate languages, the communication of animals is too simple for most rings. Your ring can translate the sounds or signals of animals into understandable words.

options.label: Avatar of the Green
options.description:
Besides the Emotional Spectrum, there is also an Elemental Spectrum which connects with the various magical elements in the universe. Some Green Lanterns can connect with [color=Green]The Green[/color], allowing them to control, grow, and communicate with non-sapient plants. 

[-4 [color=Green]Green[/color], -7 Any]

options.label: Avatar of the Red
options.description:
Besides the Emotional Spectrum, there is also an Elemental Spectrum which connects with the various magical elements in the universe. Some Red Lanterns can connect with [color=Red]The Red[/color], allowing them to control, heal, and communicate with non-sapient animals. 

Well, maybe. There is no evidence of this, but it's a logical extrapolation.

[-6 [color=Red]Red[/color], -7 Any]

options.label: Avatar of the Gray
options.description:
Besides the Emotional Spectrum, there is also an Elemental Spectrum which connects with the various magical elements in the universe. Some Gray Lanterns can connect with [color=Gray]The Gray[/color], allowing them to control, grow, and communicate with non-sapient fungi.

There is absolutely no evidence of this being true, but wouldn't it be neat?

[-6 [color=Gray]Gray[/color], -7 Any]

options.label: Life Maintenance
options.description: Your Power Ring can provide you with nutrients and remove your waste, provided you have enough energy.

options.label: Living Death
options.description: If you die, your lantern *should* fly off to find a new lantern, but instead, it will keep you in a state of undeath. Your rotting brain will leave you somewhat less effective at your job. If your Power Ring is removed or depleted, you become a corpse.

options.label: Speak to the Dead
options.description: When you are in the presence of spirits, you can communicate with them. With more energy, you can reach into the Spirit World to speak with the long dead.

options.label: Unfinished Business
options.description: Somewhere between [weight=500]Zombie Animation[/weight] and [weight=500]Resurrection[/weight], you can bring the dead back to life to complete whatever tasks they left unfinished. Once the job is done, they return to death.

options.label: Time Travel
options.description: Time travel is possible with the Power Ring, though the forward forwards or back in time a Lantern travels, the more energy it takes. Be careful when in the past. We do not need another flashpoint.

options.label: Size Changing
options.description: You can change the size of objects, with the minimum and maximum depending on your mental strength. You can decide, when the power runs out, if the items return to their normal size or are stuck inaccurately.

options.label: Transmutation
options.description: Your Power Ring can transmute matter, changing the structure and composition. Your [weight=500]Onboard Computer[/weight] will help you get the arrangement of matter correct, but this will add to the energy needs of this already energy-intensive activity.

options.label: Power Absorption
options.description:
You can steal the natural or gained superpowers found in other people, leaving them as powerless as a mortal. This is easier with modular powers; stealing from, hypothetically, a robot built to copy powers is easier than stealing from a person whose species has powers.

[-18 with Power Copying or Lantern Power Copying]

description: It's claimed that Power Rings can do literally anything, but in reality most Lantern have fairly limited set of powers. Some [weight=500]Standard Powers[/weight] are used by Lanterns from all across the Lantern Corps, some [weight=500]Characteristic Powers[/weight] are only used in specific Lanterns Corps (generally) and some [weight=500]Special Powers[/weight] are only used by unusual lanterns.

name: Weaknesses

options.label: Emotional Consistency
options.description:
You were chosen because of a strong emotional state, but if it starts to wane, the ring will leave you and find another. A moment of weakness probably won't matter, but the longer you are not feeling your chosen emotion, the more likely the Power Ring will leave you.

Can't have [color=#D5B60A]Yellow[/color] without [weight=500]Yellow Belied[/weight] or [color=indigo]Indigo[/color] without [weight=500]Compassionate Heart[/weight]

[-2 [color=Blue]Blue[/color], +5 Any]

options.label: Emotional Instability
options.description:
Orange and Violet Lanterns have trouble controlling their reigning emotion, sometimes becoming too emotional to think rationally. This can be prevented with practice or luck. Sometimes Lanterns from other Corps might be overwhelmed by their emotions.

[-4 [color=orange]Orange[/color] or [color=violet]Violet[/color], +5 Any]

options.label: Overwhelming Emotions
options.description:
Red Lanterns have trouble controlling their emotions, leaving them nearly mindless. This weakness can be removed by bathing in Blood Lake of Ysmault or by other, more esoteric methods. Rarely, Lanterns from other Corps might be overwhelmed by their emotions. 

[-8 [color=red]Red[/color], +12 Any]

options.label: Weakened by Death
options.description:
As the emissaries of Life, White Lanterns are weakened by the presence of mass death, like massacres or natural disasters.

[-2 [color=#808080]White[/color], +1 Any]

options.label: Color Weakness
options.description:
Your ring cannot directly affect objects that are the color that your ring is weak to; Most famously, Green Lanterns cannot affect yellow objects. You can only indirectly affect objects or try to find a way to change the color.

[For Red or Orange, choose Blue or Violet or take this option twice.]

[+8 [color=red]Red[/color], [color=orange]Orange[/color], [color=#D5B60A]Yellow[/color], [color=green]Green[/color], or [color=black]Black[/color]]

options.label: Mineral Weakness
options.description: Power Rings are forged from rare, naturally occurring materials; if you encounter the minerals of a color you are weak to, your ring is de-powered, leaving you with minimal options.

options.label: Wood Weakness
options.description: Your ring cannot directly affect objects made of wood. Since you work on other planets, it might not be clear what counts as wood, but you would better try indirectly affecting it just in case.

options.label: Emotional Weakness
options.description:
If you feel the emotion that corresponds to the color your color is weak to, you Power Ring becomes weaker; most famously, Green Lanterns lost power when afraid.

[For Red or Orange, choose Hope or Love or take this option twice.]

options.label: Supersonic Disruption
options.description: Your [weight=500]Constructs[/weight] are disrupted by supersonic waves, making them break faster than expected.

options.label: Ultraviolet Light Weakness
options.description:
The Power Prism is weak to ultraviolet; not the [color=#E75480]Ultraviolet[/color] of the Emotional Spectrum, but ultraviolet light in the electromagnetic spectrrum. Ultraviolet light can completely pass through the barriers and will dirupt the usage of the Power Prism.

[-4 [color=#696969]Power Prism[/color], +5 Any]

options.label: Light Weakness
options.description:
Black Lanterns are weak to White light in the Emotional Spectrum, but they are also weak to visible light in the electromagnetic spectrum. White light will burn the flesh of a Black Lantern, but it will take a large amount to defeat the Lantern's regeneration.

[-5 Black, +5 Any]

options.label: Iron Weakness
options.description: Magic tends to have a weakness to iron pure and sometimes this is true for [weight=500]Technically Magical[/weight] Power Rings. Iron can pass through your [weight=500]Constructs[/weight] and disrupt your other powers.

options.label: Heartless
options.description: The hearts of Red Lanterns are functionless with the Power Ring, with the Power Ring fulfilling the role. If their ring is removed, they die. There are methods to restart the heart while still being a Red Lantern.

options.label: Dead
options.description: Black Lanterns are created from corpses and are animated by their Power Ring. If the ring is removed, they return to unliving corpses. Some people can use Black Rings without being dead already.

options.label: No-Kill Rule
options.description:
You are not permitted to kill with your ring; any attempt to do so will cause your ring to depower. You can weaken people with your ring and then shoot them, but your supervisors won't like that and are likely to punish you.

[-5 [color=Green]Green[/color] or [color=Blue]Blue[/color], +10 Black, +3 Any]

options.label: Codependent
options.description: Though Blue Lanterns can empower Green Lanterns, this relationship goes both ways; blue Lanterns are limited to only [weight=500]Standard[/weight] and [weight=500]Characteristic Powers[/weight] when not close to a Green Lantern.

options.label: Planetary Conenction
options.description:
Some Green Lanterns who are [weight=500]Avatars of the Green[/weight] are powered by plants, so they grow weaker as they [weight=500]Fly[/weight] away from life-bearing planets. Theoretically, a Lantern could lose powers when far away from life-bearing planets, since the Emotional Spectrum is created by life.

[+3 points without [weight=500]Flight[/weight]]

options.label: Mental Instability
options.description:
It may be from stress from the job, but many Lanterns seem to be prone to mental illness.

[-3 [color=#696969]Colorless Ring[/color], +2 Any]

options.label: Unstable Option
options.description:
Your Power Ring is not as study as it seems; if you try to expend too much energy at once, it is likely to detonate in an explosion powerful enough to kill you. There will be warning signs, so do not ignore them.

[-6 [color=#696969]Fractal Lanterns[/color] or [color=#696969]Colorless Rings[/color], +8 Any]

options.label: Life Draining
options.description: Your Power Ring continually drains life from you, causing pain and deformity. You can stave it off by continually keeping it fully charged, but this won't stop it indefinitely.

description:
No Lantern is perfect and each Lantern Corps has different flaws. These flaws may be removed from hard work, lucky circumstances, or universal differences. 

[If a trait **Costs Points** for your color, you can pay to **Remove** the weakness. If a trait **Gives Points** for your color, you can gain points to **Gain** the weakness.]

name: Power Ring

subcategories.name: Trait
subcategories.text: Optional: Pick **One**. If a trait **Gives Points** for your color, you can pay to **Remove** the power. Otherwise, you can pay points to Gain the power.]

options.label: No Password Protection
options.description: Though you may have gotten your ring in special circumstances, anyone can use it if they remove it from your finger.

options.label: Anti-Theft Device
options.description: If someone tries to use your Power Ring before you die, the defense system will activate, with the lethality depending on your Lantern Corps.

options.label: Immovable Ring
options.description:
Black Power Rings imbed tendrils into the Black Lantern, making them unremovable by physical force. Violet methods must be used, but they must defeat the [weight=500]Regeneration[/weight] of the Black Lanterns.

[+1 Black, -2 Any]

options.label: Non-Physical Ring
options.description:
Ultraviolet Lanterns do not have a physical Power Ring, but instead a tattoo, making it unremovable by physical force. Removing the offending hand might just make the tattoo move 9if the Lantern survives the dismemberment].

[+1 [color=#E75480]Ultraviolet[/color], -3 Any]

subcategories.name: Attributes
subcategories.text: Optional: Pick as many as you want

options.label: Recall Feature
options.description: If your ring is removed, you can will it to return to your hand. If the ring is stuck, your mental strength must overpower the physical strength of the trap.

options.label: Remote Activation
options.description: You can set up commands for what the Power Ring should do if it is not on your hand but you are still alive. The Power Ring can use all your [weight=500]Powers[/weight], but it can't react to changing circumstances.

options.label: No Emergency Supply
options.description: All Power Rings have a supply of energy which is spent in emergencies to keep the Lantern alive. Your ring does not, giving you more effective power at the cost of your life.

options.label: Mutagenic Ring
options.description: Your Power Ring seems to be leaking, you may start to develop the ability to use your powers without your ring, as will any children you have. The won't be consistent or as reliable as your actual ring; who knows what powers you will retain?

subcategories.name: Battery
subcategories.text: Pick **One**. **Fractal Lanterns**, **Colorless Rings**, and **Power Prism** must take **Batteries not Included**

options.label: Periodic Charging
options.description: Your ring is either "charged" or "uncharged." After 24 hours, your ring goes from "charged" to "uncharged." The energy you have has no correlation with what you used the ring for.

options.label: Intensity Recharging
options.description: You need to charge your ring more often the more you use it. The Power Ring's charge can last weeks if you do low intensity acts or hours if you are busy.

options.label: Long Distance Charging
options.description: You have no Personal Power Battery and instead your ring charges passively from the Central Power Battery. Your maximum energy supply will be low and will deplete quickly, but you can recharge your ring by simply waiting patiently.

options.label: Batteries not included
options.description: You did not receive a Power Battery with your Power Ring; maybe you were recruited in unusual circumstances, maybe your ring isn't a real Power Ring. You need to use different methods to recharge.

options.label: Invisible Hiding
options.description: When you aren't using your Power Battery you can have it be invisible. Do not forget where it is.

options.label: Dimensional Hiding
options.description: When not being used, your Power Battery is hidden out of phase with the rest of the universe, meaning only you can access it. Do not leave it behind if you travel to an alternate universe such as Sector -1

options.label: Endless Storage
options.description:
If you spend your time recharging your ring, it can store beyond 100% storage. Larfleeze was able to store 100,000% charge after billions of years of hoarding.

[0 [color=orange]Orange[/color], -4 Any]

options.label: Mandatory Oath
options.description: You *must* say your Oath when recharging your Power Ring. If you change the words or cannot speak, you cannot charge your ring.

options.label: Unaging Lantern
options.description: A simplified [weight=500]Age Modification[/weight], you power Battery keeps you young when you're close to it. If your Power battery is stole or [weight=500]Hiding[/weight] in another [weight=500]Dimension[/weight], you return to your expected age.

options.label: Summoned to work
options.description: Your Power Battery will drag you to where you are needed (using [weight=500]Flight[/weight] or [weight=500]Teleportation[/weight], depending on what you have.) If the Power Battery is not in your hand at the time, you can easily track it

options.label: Color Theft
options.description: You can charge your Power Ring from the Power Battery of any Lantern, even diametrically opposed colors.

options.label: False Battery
options.description: You have managed to cobble together a Power Battery to recharge your ring, either in addition to or instead of a true Power Battery. Not being a godlike being, your Power Battery is inferior and liable to break, but it is better than nothing.

subcategories.name: Other Modifiers
subcategories.text: With an infinite universe, not every Lantern will be predictable. Sometimes, they have unique backgrounds or strange training.

options.label: Iron Lantern
options.description: You integrated your Power Battery and Ring into a suit of power armor. Your Powers are unchanged, but if your power Ring is depleted, you will still have protection from damage.

options.label: Mother Ring
options.description:
Your ring was modified with a Mother Box from New Genesis, greatly boosting all the powers it currently already has. It can also produce Boom Tubes, allowing you to travel to planets outside the universe like New Genesis or Apokolips (or anywhere in the normal universe).

[-9 Points with Bug, -8 Points with New God]

options.label: Dawnbreaker Ring
options.description: You corrupted your Power Ring with your psychological issues. Your [weight=500]constructs [/weight] are made of darkness and you can snuff out the light around you, including the [weight=500]Constructs[/weight] of other lanterns. If your ring connects with other Lantern's Power Rings, their rings will also become corrupted into Dawnbreaker Rings.

options.label: Infiltrator
options.description: Scarabs are biomechanical armors developed by the Reach, which will adapt itself to fight better. Rarely, someone can have both a Scarab and a Power Ring working together, but Scarabs are automatically hostile towards Power Rings. Particularly, Power Rings require creativity while the Scarab forces obedience to the Reach, emaning when one device gets stronger, the other gets weaker.

options.label: Alpha-Lantern
options.description: You have been cybernetically enhanced to be more durable and logical. Your Power Battery has been embedded in your chest and you can drain energy from other Green Lanterns. You do not need to eat or sleep and can undergo self-repair protocols.

options.label: Omega Lantern I
options.description:
You call yourself an Omega Lantern.

Why? Because it sounds good.

options.label: Omega Lantern II
options.description: Due to unexpected circumstances, your Power Ring was hit by an Omega Beam, allowing you access to the Omega Force. Your ring can fire Omega Beams, destructive or healing energy that can track the target. You can also use the Omega Force to power your ring, meaning that you do not need to recharge it. However, Power Rings were not built to use the Omega Force, making it hard to stabilize your powers.

options.label: Omega Lantern III
options.description: Due to unexpected circumstances, your Power Ring was hit by an Omega Beam, killing you and enslaving you to Darkseid. As the personification of evil, you can imagine being enslaved to him is bad.

options.label: Former Responsibilities
options.description: before you were chosen by your Power Ring, you were important; a noble, a high priest, a CEO. The more you help your Lantern Corps, the more annoyed your followers will be with you neglecting your duty (and vice versa)

options.label: Secret Identity
options.description: You need to keep your real identity a secret from others, for some reason. Maybe you're a known criminal, maybe you are from a disliked [weight=500]Species[/weight], maybe you have precise ideas for being a superhero. Either way, you need to do a lot of work for very little reward, and severe consequences if you fail.

options.label: Spaceship
options.description: You have a spaceship allownig to transport items at FTL speeds. You could already do this with a combination of [weight=500]Constructs[/weight], [weight=500]Flight[/weight], and [weight=500]Atmospheric Protection[/weight], but the spaceship does not use up the energy of your Power Ring.

options.label: Lantern Spaceship
options.description:
You have a spaceship designed by your Lantern Corps, which uses a massive Power Battery for energy, allowing the power systems to be compatible with your Power Ring. The Lantern Corps designed the Interceptor first, but other Lantern Corps could do the same.

[-6 [color=green]Green[/color], -7 Any]

description: The Power Rings are the focus for all powers of a Lantern and are nearly indestructible. They can reshape themselves to fit any species or even transform into different objects. Besides that, they may have special functions.

subcategories.name: Duties
subcategories.text:
Unlike many superheroes and supervillains, Lanterns do not get to do whatever they want. Instead, they are assigned jobs and can have their powers revoked if they misbehave. Except, of course, for the Lanterns who act differently.

Choose One option

options.label: Average Lantern
options.description: You would be considered a quitessential Lantern for your Lantern Corps. YOu are assigned a [weight=500]Sector[/weight], where you patrol to achieve the [weight=500]Goals[/weight] of your [weight=500]Corps[/weight]. Your supervisors may give you special assignments or send you help, but generally they trust you to do you job.

options.label: Paragon
options.description: You are considered the peak of the Lanterns, due to your heroic actions before. You are granted a lot of leeway in your actions, and your supervisors will gladly send you assistance if you ask. However, if you mess up, your punishment will be even more severe.

options.label: Irritation
options.description: You becoming a Lantern was not a popular choice amongst your supervisors; perhaps you are disobedient or you are a former member of an enemy Lantern Corps. Your supervisors will be watching you carefully; not to help you, of course, but waiting for you to make the wrong move so they can eject you.

options.label: Planetary Assignment
options.description: In some unusual circumstances, Lanterns are assigned a single planet or star system instead of an entire Sector. You won;t be provided an assistance; if you have all of your powers and still cannot handle one planet, you are not fit to be a Lantern.

options.label: Management
options.description:
The Lantern Corps are big organizations and they need someone to deal with the minutiae of running it. You might be a drill sergeant, administrator, researcher, cook, or other such jobs. You won't be having adventures and you will rarely use your powers, but you can still take pride in your job.

[Skips Nemesis]

options.label: Internal Affairs
options.description:
Lanterns are extremely powerful and the Lantern Corps need someone to make sure this power is not being misused. Like the Alpha-Lanterns of the Green Lantern Corps or the Black Circle of the Sinestro Corps, you observe other Lanterns to make sure they are obeying the mission of the Corps.

[Skips Nemesis]

options.label: Honor Guard
options.description:
The Honor Guard are assigned to the headquarters of the Lantern Corps, where they protect the base and leaders. Though they rarely use their powers, when they do, it is a fight for their lives, only the most powerful enemies will be able to reach the headquarters.

[Skips Nemesis]

options.label: Black Ops
options.description: Though the Corps have their stated mission, sometimes they need to break their rules to get the job done. Like the Corpse of the Green Lantern Corps, you are sent on missions without the moral limitations of other Lanterns. You work in secrecy, with no evidence of your loyalty and your supervisors denying your existence.

options.label: Possessed by the Entity
options.description: You are possessed by the personification of your color, which is both an honor and a duty. You will find your powers boosted, but if you act against the goal of the Corps or entity, it will either leave you (like Adara) or force you to obey (like Parallax)

options.label: Possessed by the Predator
options.description: Despite the Star Sapphire Corps acting like a rational Lantern Corps, the Violet personification is one of the most unstable. Once possessed by the Predator, your powers will be boosted, but you act like every negative lover stereotypes towards your chosen partner: Selfish, Controlling, Possessive, and Violent

options.label: Marching in File
options.description: Most Indigo Lanterns travel in nomadic bands, searching out people lacking in Compassion and forcing a Power Ring onto them. Indigo Lanterns also reject selfishness and individuality, so they work in the group, helping each other.

options.label: Spreading Out
options.description: Some Indigo Lanterns can work independently; generally Lanterns with a [weight=500]Compassionate Soul[/weight] or Lanterns who have shown considerable remorse. Some assist Lanterns from other Corps, while some go on personal missions.

options.label: Minions of the Horde
options.description:
Most Black, Ultraviolet, and Red Lanterns are nearly mindless and simply follow one of the Heralds. Their job tends to be equally simple, attacking whoever the Herald commands.

[Skips Nemesis]

options.label: Herald of the Horde
options.description: There are methods to retain your intelligence despite being a Black, Ultraviolet, or Red Lantern; tohse Lanterns that do so lead the other Lanterns. Though still a slave to the obligations of your Lantern Corps, you can make decisions and have enough intelligence to taunt your enemies.

options.label: Possessed by the Entity
options.description: It should not surprise you to learn that being possessed by Nekron, Umbrax, or the Butcher is not a pleasant experience. Though your powers are increased, you are little more than a slave.

options.label: Mine, Mine, Mine
options.description: As Agent Orange, it is absurd to think that someone could order you around. Your only responsibility is to add to your collection.

options.label: Odd Jobs
options.description: You are given tasks by the Life Entity, though they do not always seem to be obviously useful. Sometimes, it will be logical like fighting Black Lanterns, but sometimes it is strange like burning down a forest or throwing a weapon at someone.

options.label: Possessed by the Life Entity
options.description: You are possessed by the personification of life, which is both an honor and a duty. The Life Entity seems strangely fickle, granting you vast powers until it does not. It will push you into the right direction to follow its goals.

options.label: Why shouldn't I keep it?
options.description: Since you have no supervisors to command you, you can do whatever you want with your phenomenal power. Whether other people will allow you is another matter.

options.label: New Corps, Donut Steel
options.description: Just because you do not have a Lantern Corps now does not mean you cannot make one. You have started building a new Lantern Corps to rival the others. All you need is a Central Power Battery, Personal Power Batteries, a way to make new Power Rings, a way to find recruits, a Book of Lore, a clearly defined mission statement, a home, ...

options.label: Set Right What Went Wrong
options.description: You have been sent back in time to solve some emergency with the timeline. You have some records of the critical points in history, so you travel around trying to fix the issues.

options.label: Ontological Paradox
options.description: You found your ring/orb after it went back in time and your only job is to eventually learn how to [weight=500]Time Travel[/weight], so that you can send it back in time for you to find. Do not forget about it or you will tear a hole in the universe.

options.label: Second Chance
options.description: Though you do not have anyone to teach you, you did your best to fulfill the mission of your Lantern Corps. You guessed at what a [weight=500]Sector[/weight] would be, who would need to be punished and what is a suitable response. You are not perfect, but you are doing your best.

options.label: He Choose Poorly
options.description: Whoever gave you the last Power Ring made a bad decision. Despite your amazing cosmic powers and the precedence of your Lantern Corps, you do not seem to be using your powers responsibly.

options.label: Independent
options.description: With no one to guide you, you decided to forge your own path. Someday, you may encounter another Lantern of the same color, but for now, you work alone.

options.label: Starbreaker's Squad
options.description: [weight=500]Luciphage the Starbreaker[/weight] has claimed that he will create a Power Battery that will allow you to recharge and you believed him. You will be sent across the universe to find the material to craft this Master Battery. Strangely, the other Lanterns seem to respond badly to this.

options.label: Exchange Program
options.description: Though you *should* be working for a Lantern Corps, you decided to join another one. This will inevitably be a temporary job; either you do not fit in and will be rejected or you do fit in and will be given a more appropriate Power Ring.
options.prerequisites: actionsDutiesAverageLantern || actionsDutiesIrritation || actionsDutiesPlanetaryAssignment || actionsDutiesManagement || actionsDutiesMarchingInFile || actionsDutiesHeraldOfTheHorde || actionsDutiesOddJobs

options.label: Temporary Recruit
options.description: You are not a good fir for your Lantern Corps, but everyone knows that; you were just recruited to fill out the numbers. You only have to work for a few days before the ring leaves you and finds someone more suitable. Perhaps you can convince your supervisors to extend the deadline, but probably not.

options.label: New Guardians
options.description: You do not have a normal job, as you have been selected to work in a [weight=500]Lantern Alliance[/weight]. This coalition is formed to deal with crises that threaten the entire universe and are optimally made of seven Lanterns of the seven basic colors (but desperate times may lead to desperate measures.)

options.label: Stolen Valor
options.description: You stole your Power Ring, so you are not beholden to anyone. You can do whatever you want and claim any background yo uwant. This does mean you will be hunted down by Lanterns who are *actually* members of your Lantern Corps.

options.label: Fraud
options.description: It is odd that you were chosen to wield a Power Ring, was it not? Well, it is because it is a fake ring, given to you to make your Lantern Corps look bad. These rings will eventually break, so your options are either convince your superiors that you deserve a real ring or enjoy your time left.

options.label: Ignorant and Lost
options.description: Through very unusual circumstances, you have no idea what a Lantern is and therefore cannot obey the mission of your Lantern Corps is, what sectors are or how a Power Battery works.

subcategories.name: Sector
subcategories.text: The Guardian of the Universe split their territory (the Oan Protectorate) into 3600 sectors, with every standard sector being a pyramid-shaped wedge with the point centered on the planet Oa (Sector 0) and spreading out from there. The exact size of each Sector depends on the universe, but there is always a vast amount of territory to cover. Outside the Oan Protectorate is uncharted territory known as the Unknown Sectors or Frontier Space.

options.label: Corps Headquarters
options.description: Instead of being assigned a Sector, some Lanterns work at their Lantern Corps Headquarters. Though, they are still busy, they tend not to encounter the threats of the rest of the Sector.

options.label: Sector -1
options.description: Sector -1 is in the paired antimatter universe, with Qward being the equivalent to Oa. The Weaponeers of Qward are old enemies of the Guardians of the Universe, so most Lanterns avoid Sector -1. However, the Weaponeers originally crafted the Power Rings of the Sinestro Corps, so they are sometimes found there.

options.label: Sector 17
options.description:
Sector 17 was considered one of the most violent and crime-ridden Sectors, especially the planet Garnet. 

The planet Sputa is alright, though.

options.label: Sector 119
