options.description: Sector 119 was an unremarkable Sector, until the Yellow Lantern Despotellis infected the Sector, leading to the plague that exterminated 85% of the population. This led to anarchy in the Sector; the only reason it did not get worse is because criminals were also killed off.

options.label: Sector 650
options.description:
Sector 650 is a dark Sector infested with vampires. With their burning blood, control over corpses, and strength over death, respecitively, Red, Black, and White Lanterns have an advantage over the vampires

[-1 [color=Red]Red[/color], [color=Black]Black[/color], or [color=#808080]White[/color], +1 Any]

options.label: Sector 666
options.description: The Manhunters were a prototype for the Green Lantern Corps, which eventually malfunctioned and exterminated all life in Sector 666. This massacre was the origin of the Red Lantern Corps and the reason why Nekron put the Black Lantern Corps Headquarters in Sector 666. Besides the Black Lantern Corps Headquarters in Sector 666, the only people in Sector 666 are scavengers and the lost.

options.label: Sector 1287
options.description:
Sector 1287 is protected by the Green Lantern the Puffball Collective, a hive mind of billions of spores. This leaves Sector 1287 as one of the safest Sectors.

[+3 [color=#E75480]Ultraviolet[/color] or [color=Black]Black[/color], -2 Any]

options.label: Sector 1634
options.description: The Green Lantern of Sector 1634 was an extremely hands-on micromanager and so the inhabitants of the sector treated him like a god. Once he was killed by his son, Lord Malvolio, and the next Green Lantern resigned, the sector fell into chaos.

options.label: Sector 2814
options.description:
As it contains Earth, Sector 2814 is choked with superheroes and supervillains. It also contains the headquarters of Ysmault and Nok, leaving it even more crowded with Lanterns. You really want to add to the congestion? 

[0 Points with Copernican Principle or Infinite Crises on Infinite Worlds]

options.label: Sector 2828
options.description: Sector 2828 contains the Vega system, which is where the Orange Power Battery was found by Agent Orange. The contract between Agent Orange and the Guardians of the Galaxy meant Green Lanterns were forbidden from entering the Vega system, leaving it a hotspot for crime.

options.label: Sector 3600
options.description: Though Sector 2600 was once an ordinary Sector, as the God T:D:H:D grew in power, it eventually reached the point that fused with the universe itself and killed all life in the Sector. The Mad God was imprisoned by the Guardians of the Universe, leaving Sector 3600 lifeless. The only people in Sector 3600 are scavengers and the lost.

options.label: Sector 3601
options.description: Sector 3601 is outside the territory of Oan Protectorate.After exterminating all life in Sector 666, the surviving Manhunters retreated to Biot in Sector 3601. The Manhunters are erratic in their programming, but it is likely they will kill anyone who enters their Sector, especially lanterns.

options.label: Other Sectors
options.description: There are 3591 more sectors that you may be assigned to; there's no way to list them all.

options.label: No Sector (Mobile)
options.description: You have no Sector, as you travel throughout the universe to solve problems wherever they pop up. Everywhere you go will be unknown territory and you will never be able to keep allies.

options.label: No Sector (Deadbeat)
options.description: You have no sector, as you have no supervisors or you ignore them. You go wherever you like ignoring or solving any problem you encounter.

subcategories.name: Complications
subcategories.text:
Though there may be famous planets within a Sector, there are still millions or billions of other planets that do not draw enough atetntion. Sometimes, there are unsuual events or inhabitants that the Lanterns need to deal with.

Take a maximum of 5 (or a maximum of three with Planetary Assignment). Skip with Corps Headquarters or No Sector

options.label: Allied Lantern
options.description:
You share a Sector with another Lantern from either the same Lantern Corps or from a Lantern Corps with similar goals. You do not always work together, but you know you can get assistance from someone equally as powerful as you.

[The [weight=500]Allied Lantern[/weight] can be created with this CYOA with 50 points or can be assumed to be an average Lantern.]

options.label: Rival Lantern
options.description:
You share a Sector with another Lantern from a Lantern Corps with antithetical goals. You will constantly come into conflict, but on matters threatening existence, you can at least get assistance then.

[The [weight=500]Rival Lanterns[/weight] can be created with this CYOA with 50 points or can be assumed to be an average Lantern.]

options.label: The Hand of Krona
options.description: The Hand of Krona worships Krona, a Guardian of the Universe who corrupted the structure of the universe by watching its creation. The cult believes that this was a good thing and spends their time upgrading the universe with technology. They do not care about the opinions of the people they are cybernetically enhancing and their actions are slipshod and may cause more problems than they cause.

options.label: Darkstar Jurisdiction
options.description:
The Darkstars considers much of your sector as their territory. The Darkstars are a peacekeeping force founded by the Controllers, a branch of the Malthusians, Though their Exosuits provide superpowers, they have no connection to the Emotional Spectrum. Lawful Lantern Corps tend to suffer from jurisdiction friction, while more criminal ones are hunted down.

[+3 [color=Red]Red[/color], [color=orange]Orange[/color], [color=#D5B60A]Yellow[/color], [color=#E75480]Ultraviolet[/color], or [color=Black]Black[/color], +1 Any]

options.label: Space Canine Patrol Agency Jurisdiction
options.description:
The Space Canine Patrol Agency considers much of your Sector their territory. The SCPA is a crime-fighting organization with super-powered members, but all those members are gods. They mainly concern themselves with dog-based crimes, but they my be involved with your actions.

[+1 [color=Red]Red[/color], [color=orange]Orange[/color], [color=#D5B60A]Yellow[/color], [color=#E75480]Ultraviolet[/color], [color=Black]Black[/color], -1 Any]

options.label: Space Pirates
options.description: Space Pirates are active in your Sector. All Lanterns appreciate space pirates. None are strong enough to fight a Lantern, but there's always a bounty on them.

options.label: Sun-Eater
options.description:
There is a Sun-Eater wandering through you Sector. As the name suggests, Sun-Eaters feed on entire stars. Killing a Sun-Eater is unlikely. Hal Jordan, infused with the entire energy of the Green Lantern Corps, died killing one. It might be easier to lure it towards uninhabited star systems.

[-5 [color=Black]Black[/color], +5 Any]

options.label: Terravore
options.description:
Terravores re planet eaters, but they farm planets to get the right taste. They can mind control the inhabitants to get them to reshape the planet's surface until they slice it up for eating. If a Lantern tries to interfere, they have comparable cosmic powers to fight back.

[+6 with Planetary Assignment]

options.label: Doomsday
options.description:
Doomsday is causing destruction on one of the planets of your Sector. Doomsday is a nigh-unkillable Krpytonian bioweapon. Though you could potentially kill him, he will just reanimate immune to however you killed him. Potentially, you could find a non-violent way to stop him from killing (or just leave him alone and let him kill only one planet.)

[+5 with Planetary Assignment]

[-3 Black, +3 Any]

options.label: Lobo
options.description: Lobo, the last Czarnian, is drifting through your Sector. He is an intergalactic bounty hunter and impossible to kill, but at the moment he is between jobs. He is not hunting you now, but he is prone to rash decisions and is a mean drunk.

options.label: Event Horizon
options.description: The Event Horizon is an endless intergalactic rave run by Kindred Marx. It never stays in one place for long and is currently traveling through your Sector. Normally, a race is beneath the notice of Lanterns, but Event Horizon seems to attract a lot of superhuman visitors and they get very rowdy. Still, it's somewhere to relax between tasks.

options.label: Kaiju
options.description: At least one of the planets in your Sector is inhabited by a building-sized creatures. Each individual Kaiju tends to be unique, but most are gigantic and dangerous. They can be massively destructive when angered, but will depend their planet when invaded and are potential Lantern recruits.

options.label: High-String Populations
options.description: You remember the [weight=500]Fractal Lanterns[/weight] and how they can appear wherever someone is feeling strong emotions? That includes the planets in your Sector. This can happen anywhere and every new Fractal Lantern is unstable and dangerous to others. There could even be the formation of brand new emotion/color combinations.

options.label: Metabeings
options.description: People with superpowers keeping cropping up in your worlds in your Sector, likely caused by a metagene, Gen-Factor or X-gene. Unlike [weight=500]Superpowered Species[/weight], these "Metabeings" occur in otherwise mundane species and vary widely in powers and abilities. Thankfully, less than 1% of the population, but on more populated planets, this can lead to millions of metabeings.

options.label: Doomed Homeworld
options.description:
There are reports that one of the populated worlds in your Sector is going to explode. This is not a natural act for planets, so it is probably a deliberate attack. You will need to find the cause, the solution, and a way to stop it from happening again. 

[+5 Planetary Assignment]

[-3 Black, +3 Any]

options.label: Bloodline Parasite
options.description: The Bloodline Parasite are inter-dimensional monsters who entered the universe in your Sector. They harvest the spinal fluids of people to reproduce. If the target does not die, they gain random superpowers. They can shapeshift to disguise themselves and each has individual powers. If you do not have enough trouble dealing with the Parasites, the people with sudden superpowers will be a problem.

options.label: Black Mercy Infestation
options.description: There are Black Mercies growing on planets in your sector (possibly spread by seeds on the boots of travelers) . Black Mercies can entangle people and trap them in dreams to feed on their psyhic emissions. You will need to be careful when traveling or try to find the Green Lantern Mother Mercy, the sapient progenitor.

options.label: Krolotean Experiments
options.description: Kroloteans are a diminutive race known for their scientific experimentation on other races. They would pose little threat to you personally: They are weak and their technology is focused on experimentation and stealth. Instead, their creations are the threat. Even discarded, specimens can slaughter cities. Kroloteans are active in your Sector, trying to make the perfect soldier to sell to other buyers.

options.label: Spider Guild Expansion
options.description:
The Spider Guild is an expansionist coalition. They send fleets of drones to find weak planets; once found, they strip the planet of resources and harvest the inhabitants for food. There are thousands of spider drones searching through your Sector. Each force is fragile, but they are happy to overwhelm you.

[-2 [color=Black]Black[/color], +2 Any]

options.label: Xenomorph Infestation
options.description:
Xenomorphs are a parasitoid, eusocial species of unknown origin. Xenomorph eggs hatch Facehuggers, which implant Chestbursters into any creatures, which grow into Drones, which may develop into Queens which lay eggs. Singular Xenomorphs are stalking threats while Xenomorph swarms can overwhelm cities. There are Xenomorphs somewhere in your Sector. They will be hard to find until there are overwhelming numbers. 

[-1 [color=Black]Black[/color], +1 Any]

subcategories.name: Nemesis
subcategories.text: Power creates resentment and envy, so Lanterns are always coming into conflict with others. With your vast powers, most will be easily defeated, but a few are a class above the others. They can have powers matching or even surpassing a Lantern and might even return after death.

options.label: Bolphunga the Unrelenting
options.description: Bolphunga the Unrelenting is a mercenary with a hatred for the Green Lantern Corps. He is also an indiot and has no superpowers, so is only threatening to Lanterns who do not have their Power Ring.

options.label: Despero
options.description: Despero is the tyrannical ruler of Kalanor. He originally just had a range of psychic powers like hypnosis, illusions, and telekinesis, but he was enhanced by the Flame of Pytar, giving him enough strenght and power to fight a Kryptonian. He is most often seen off-planet hunting down defectors or expanding his territory, but he is heavily motivated by spite. If you defeat him once, he will continually return to seek his revenge. He will also try different schemes each tie, using his mind, strenght, or advanced technology.

options.label: Evil Star
options.description: Evil Star is a scientist from the planet Aoran who invented the "Star-Band" to gain immortality. The Star-Band harvests the energy of stars to prolong Evil Star's life, allow flight, create force blasts or hard-light constructs and generate Starlings (miniatures copies of Evil Star). Though Evil Star has conflicting morals, he fears death most of all. He will do anything he can to keep his Star-Brands and make sure he can continue harvesting stars.

options.label: Kanjar Ro
options.description: Kanjar Ro is the dictator of the planet Dhor in Sector 2262. He carries an Energi-Rod that allows him to fly and communicate through hyper-space, as well as Gamma Gong which can paralyze others when struck, He has been fighting the other planets in his system (Alstair, Mosteel and LLarr) for decades and is always looking for an advantage. He believes there is something in your sector that can help him.

options.label: Starro
options.description: Starro is an ancient [weight=500]Star Conqueror[/weight], possibly the progenitor of the race. He enslaves planets to mind control people, but his age means he is far more practiced than other [weight=500]Star Conquerors[/weight] and has more powerful psychic abilities. People with superpowers retain them when controlled so he will try to infest you first before invading the planets in your sector.

options.label: Mongul
options.description: The Mongul lineage is generations of warlords who control Warworld, a mobile weaponized satellite with enough power to obliterate a planet. Though each is strong enough to fight an empowered Kryptonian, some are cunning as well. One of the Mongul trying to expand his territory and sees you a teh most obvious roadblock. Some Monguls have learned the strength of Power Rings and have enough emotional depth to wield them.

options.label: Luciphage the Starbreaker
options.description: Somehow related to a [weight=500]Star-Eater[/weight], Luciphage feeds on all energy, including the energy of the Emotional Spectrum. When well fed, he has superhuman strength and durability, flight, size alteration, psychic abilities, and energy blasts. He has had many schemes over the yers to obtain more energy, most of them thwarted by the Green Lanterns.

options.label: Superwoman
options.description: Luma Lynai is a superhero from the planet Staryl. She has the powers of a Kryptonian though she will lose those powers when exposed to the light of a yellow sun. She has a strong moral fiber, so she will only come in conflict with you if you are committing criminal acts.

options.label: Vengeful Lantern
options.description: You are being hunted by another Lantern, though the reason depends on circumstances. If you are a thief, it is obvious a Lantern trying to get back the stolen property. If you are from [weight=500]Internal Affairs[/weight], it might be a Lantern who is angry at being caught. Otherwise, it could be a Lantern from a rival Lantern Corps who is ruled up by your actions.

options.label: Lantern Alliance
options.description: You are a universe-threatening villain, so an alliance of Lanterns from the different Lantern Corps have been formed to stop you. Most Lantern alliances involve one Lantern from each of the 7 basic colors, but it is possible your particular actions agitate a different set of Lanterns. Though they may bicker and fight, they know you must be stopped at any cost.
